summary.brief: Walterboro Community Hosp. v. Meacher. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: On March 10, 2000, Grant arrived at the emergency department at Colleton, complaining of pain and swelling in his left testicle.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Grant was examined and treated by Dr. Meacher, who, according to Grant's amended complaint, diagnosed Grant with epididymitis and released him.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He thereafter sought treatment at the Medical University of South Carolina ("MUSC"), where he was diagnosed with testicular torsion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The MUSC physicians determined that Grant's testicle could not be repaired, and it was surgically removed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Grant subsequently sued Colleton, Dr. Meacher, Meacher P.A., and CareFirst for medical malpractice.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Grant's case proceeded to trial. On the second day of trial, Grant reached a settlement with Colleton, Dr. Meacher, and Meacher P.A. for $100,000.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The circuit court subsequently found that Colleton was not entitled to equitable indemnification and that it was not entitled to recovery under the Agreement.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We believe the preponderance of the evidence supports the conclusion that Colleton failed to meet the Vermeer requirements.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: At the Grant trial, Dr. Mazo testified that Dr. Meacher's failure to order an ultrasound was a departure from [the] standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: There was insufficient evidence of fault on Dr. Meacher's part and lack of fault on Colleton's part presented at the medical malpractice trial prior to settlement.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain and swelling in left testicle
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports pain and swelling in left testicle upon arrival at the emergency department.

focused_exam.genitourinary: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

critical_findings: Pain and swelling in left testicle

requestables.name: Ultrasound of the scrotum
requestables.description: Ultrasound to evaluate testicular blood flow and structure

reveal.decision_impact: Failure to perform led to misdiagnosis

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with testicular pain and swelling
decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic test should be ordered?

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard investigation to rule out testicular torsion
Assumed diagnosis without ruling out torsion

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant assumed epididymitis based on symptoms

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated ultrasound was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order ultrasound led to misdiagnosis

patient_outcome.description: Patient's testicle was surgically removed due to testicular torsion

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to order ultrasound
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have prevented loss of testicle

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Ultrasound would have shown testicular torsion
